spring-boot-2-jpa-spring-data-rest/src/m.general_idea: Migrate JPA annotations from javax.persistence to jakarta.persistence package.
spring-boot-2-jpa-spring-data-rest/src/m.why_exists: javax.persistence package is removed in Jakarta EE 9+ (Spring Boot 3+); code fails to compile with 'package javax.persistence does not exist'.

spring-boot-2-jpa-spring-data-rest/src/m.general_idea: Add @NonNull annotations to generic type parameters to satisfy stricter null-safety requirements in Spring Boot 4 / JDK 21.
spring-boot-2-jpa-spring-data-rest/src/m.why_exists: Spring Boot 4.0.0-M1 (and underlying Spring Data) enforces stricter null-safety checks on repository interfaces; omitting @NonNull on generic types may cause compilation errors or runtime validation failures regarding nullable type parameters.

spring-boot-2-jpa-spring-data-rest/src/m.general_idea: Import the @NonNull annotation from org.jspecify to support the updated generic type declarations.
spring-boot-2-jpa-spring-data-rest/src/m.why_exists: The @NonNull annotation used in the interface definition is not available without this import, causing a compilation error.

spring-boot-2-jpa-spring-data-rest/src/t.general_idea: Replace JUnit 4 import with JUnit 5 import for the @Test annotation.
spring-boot-2-jpa-spring-data-rest/src/t.why_exists: org.junit.Test is not available in JUnit 5; using it causes a compile error if the JUnit 4 dependency is removed or if the project enforces JUnit 5.

spring-boot-2-jpa-spring-data-rest/src/t.general_idea: Replace JUnit 4 @RunWith annotation with JUnit 5 @ExtendWith annotation.
spring-boot-2-jpa-spring-data-rest/src/t.why_exists: @RunWith is a JUnit 4 specific annotation and is not recognized by the JUnit 5 engine, leading to test execution failures or compile errors if JUnit 4 is excluded.

spring-boot-2-jpa-spring-data-rest/src/t.general_idea: Replace JUnit 4 SpringRunner import with JUnit 5 SpringExtension import.
spring-boot-2-jpa-spring-data-rest/src/t.why_exists: SpringRunner is part of the JUnit 4 integration; SpringExtension is the JUnit 5 equivalent. Using the wrong one causes compile errors or runtime test failures.

spring-boot-2-jpa-with-hibernate-and-h2/.general_idea: Add @NonNull annotations to generic type parameters of JpaRepository to satisfy stricter null-safety requirements in the upgraded Spring Boot 4 / JDK 21 environment.
spring-boot-2-jpa-with-hibernate-and-h2/.why_exists: Spring Boot 4.0.0-M1 (and associated Spring Data versions) likely enforces stricter null-safety checks or uses annotations that require explicit non-null declarations for generic types to avoid compilation errors or runtime warnings regarding nullable types in repository interfaces.
spring-boot-2-jpa-with-hibernate-and-h2/.before: public interface StudentRepository extends JpaRepository<Student, Long>{
spring-boot-2-jpa-with-hibernate-and-h2/.after: public interface StudentRepository extends JpaRepository<@NonNull Student, @NonNull Long> {

spring-boot-2-jpa-with-hibernate-and-h2/.general_idea: Import the @NonNull annotation from org.jspecify to support the null-safety annotations added to the repository interface.
spring-boot-2-jpa-with-hibernate-and-h2/.why_exists: The code will fail to compile because the @NonNull symbol used in the generic type parameters is not defined without this import.

spring-boot-2-myBatis-with-h2/src/main/j.general_idea: Update logger variable references to match the new uppercase constant name.
